key_insight: PEFT methods should be judged not only by task accuracy, but by how well they balance adaptation with retention of pretrained capabilities.
engineering_relevance: For large-model adaptation in computational science and engineering, this highlights which finetuning choices preserve general-purpose reliability while still meeting downstream task goals.
method: PEFT-Arena benchmark jointly measures downstream performance and capability retention.
Compares PEFT methods through stability-plasticity trade-offs under matched parameter budgets.
Uses spectral analysis in weight space and retention metrics in activation space to explain forgetting.
evidence_signal: Across methods and evaluation settings, the abstract reports distinct stability-plasticity profiles and identifies orthogonal finetuning as achieving the strongest Pareto frontier under comparable parameter budgets.
watch_item: Reported gains depend on the benchmark design and parameter budget, so results may not transfer unchanged to other model families or tasks.
paper_title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
